segments.title: Platform & Infrastructure
segments.description: Compute, networking, cloud providers, Kubernetes/orchestration, edge, and HA/DR foundations. Aligned with AWS/Azure Well-Architected frameworks.
segments.label: Quadrant 1

segments.title: Application Architecture
segments.description: Integration patterns, API strategy, microservices vs. monolith, modularisation, and event-driven design. Aligned with TOGAF / ISO 42010.
segments.label: Quadrant 2

segments.title: Data & Analytics
segments.description: Persistence (SQL/NoSQL), event streaming, AI/ML models, data governance, and analytics platforms. Aligned with DAMA-DMBOK.
segments.label: Quadrant 3

segments.title: Security & Compliance
segments.description: Identity (IAM), encryption, Zero Trust, secrets management, and regulatory controls (GDPR/SOC 2). Aligned with NIST / ISO 27001.
segments.label: Quadrant 4

segments.title: Development Experience
segments.description: Languages, frameworks, CI/CD pipelines, testing tools, and developer portals. Aligned with DORA metrics / Team Topologies.
segments.label: Quadrant 5

segments.title: Operations & Reliability
segments.description: Observability (logging/tracing/metrics), SRE practices, FinOps, incident management, and chaos engineering. Aligned with the Google SRE Book.
segments.label: Quadrant 6

rings.title: Standard
rings.description: The Golden Path. Recommended for all new workloads within the defined scope. This is the default choice unless a documented exemption exists.

rings.title: Trial
rings.description: Low-risk production use. Limited to specific projects or early-adopter teams to prove value before broader adoption.

rings.title: Assess
rings.description: Currently under research or proof-of-concept. No production impact allowed yet. Evaluate feasibility, cost, and risk before proceeding to Trial.

rings.title: Sunset
rings.description: Formally deprecated. No new implementations allowed. Existing workloads must have a documented migration or exit plan.

labels.title: Technology Decision Records
labels.footer: The Gunvor Group Technology Decision Records capture technology choices across six categories and four lifecycle stages — from global directives set by the Technology Advisory Group (TAG) to project-level decisions made by engineering teams. Filter by scope-enterprise, scope-program, or scope-project tags.
labels.notUpdated: This decision record was not updated in the last three versions of the registry. If it appeared in a recent edition, it likely remains relevant. For older records, the current status may differ.
labels.metaDescription: Browse the Gunvor Group Technology Decision Records — from enterprise-wide mandates by the Technology Advisory Group to project-level architecture decisions, spanning platform, architecture, data, security, developer experience, and operations domains.
